walking_skeleton_strategy: C-local — real SQLAlchemy + in-memory SQLite (no compose stack required; the repository layer's real adapter is just the AsyncSession against an in-memory SQLite engine, mirroring backend/tests/conftest.py)

phases.title: Phase 00 — Walking skeleton green: Project aggregate split with parity proof
phases.scope: DESIGN's Phase A scoped down to ONE aggregate (Project) so the walking-skeleton scenario goes green end-to-end. Implement: (a) backend/app/repositories/metadata/_base.py — lift handle_repository_exceptions decorator out of repository.py; (b) backend/app/repositories/metadata/project_repository.py — new ProjectRepository class plus narrow ProjectRepositoryProtocol covering list_projects/get_project/create_project/update_project/delete_project/project_exists; (c) backend/app/repositories/metadata/_legacy_facade.py — _LegacyMetadataFacade class that delegates Project methods to ProjectRepository (other aggregates still routed to the unsplit MetadataRepository for now); (d) backend/app/repositories/__init__.py — register `project_repository` key, add `.projects` @property, retarget `metadata_repository` key to construct _LegacyMetadataFacade, keep `.metadata` property pointing at the facade; (e) backend/app/repositories/metadata/__init__.py — export ProjectRepository + ProjectRepositoryProtocol. The existing repository.py still owns the other 7 aggregates; the facade composes ProjectRepository for project-shaped calls and the legacy MetadataRepository for everything else. Wire the acceptance suite's repository_container fixture in tests/acceptance/refactor-metadata-repository-split/conftest.py: replace the pytest.skip stubs with a real session-scoped aiosqlite engine + AsyncSession + RestrictedSession + RepositoryContainer (mirroring backend/tests/conftest.py's db_session fixture). Replace every DISTILL pytest.fail body in steps/refactor_steps.py for the walking-skeleton steps (background, given_organization_exists, when_create_project_via_*, then_projects_match_shape, then_projects_readable, then_updates_persist, then_deletes_remove) with real implementations.
scenarios_to_unskip: tests/acceptance/refactor-metadata-repository-split/project-repository-matches-legacy-facade-end-to-end.feature :: Project create-read-update-delete returns identical results through new repo and legacy facade
phases.files_changed_estimate: backend/app/repositories/metadata/_base.py — NEW, ~30 LOC (lift the decorator). backend/app/repositories/metadata/project_repository.py — NEW, ~120 LOC (5 methods + Protocol). backend/app/repositories/metadata/_legacy_facade.py — NEW, ~80 LOC (Project delegations now; grows in Phase 01). backend/app/repositories/__init__.py — EDIT, +20 LOC (one new key, one new property, retarget metadata_repository key). backend/app/repositories/metadata/__init__.py — EDIT, +5 LOC (export new symbols). backend/app/repositories/metadata/repository.py — UNCHANGED in this phase (still owns 7 aggregates; will be progressively gutted in Phase 01). backend/tests/repositories/conftest.py — EDIT, ~10 LOC (swap MetadataRepository(...) for ProjectRepository(...) in the 4 repo fixtures so existing test_project_repository.py keeps green). tests/acceptance/refactor-metadata-repository-split/conftest.py — REWRITE, replace pytest.skip stubs with real engine+container fixtures, ~80 LOC. tests/acceptance/refactor-metadata-repository-split/steps/refactor_steps.py — EDIT, replace ~10 pytest.fail step bodies with real implementations (walking-skeleton steps only; milestone-1 + milestone-2 step bodies stay scaffolded and skipped via @pending).
exit_criteria: tests/acceptance/refactor-metadata-repository-split/project-repository-matches-legacy-facade-end-to-end.feature scenario green end-to-end (`cd tests/acceptance/refactor-metadata-repository-split && uv run --no-project pytest -m 'walking_skeleton'`)
Existing backend/tests/repositories/test_project_repository.py suite green after the conftest swap (`cd backend && uv run pytest tests/repositories/test_project_repository.py`)
Whole backend test suite green: `cd backend && uv run pytest` — facade routes correctly for the 7 unsplit aggregates
DeprecationWarning emitted on first .metadata access (verifiable by smoke import + warnings.catch_warnings)

Reviewer (nw-software-crafter-reviewer) approves: Mandate 1 (drives through RepositoryContainer, never imports ProjectRepository in step glue), no test-body edits in backend/tests/repositories/test_project_repository.py beyond the conftest fixture swap (Iron Rule honoured)
ac_references: ADR-020 §Decision outcome step 1 (lift decorator), step 2 (per-aggregate module), step 5 (container property + facade), step 6 (test conftest swap)
wave-decisions.md DWD-2 (transitional facade), DWD-3 (RestrictedSession composition), DWD-5 (helper relocation), DWD-1 here (real-IO via SQLite)

phases.title: Phase 01 — Split remaining 7 aggregates (one parameterised milestone)
phases.scope: Apply the structural mechanism established by Phase 00 to the seven remaining aggregates: Dataset, Transform, Session, View, Report, Organization, ProjectMemory. For each: (a) new per-aggregate module under backend/app/repositories/metadata/<aggregate>_repository.py with class + narrow Protocol, (b) new RepositoryContainer property (.datasets / .transforms / .sessions / .views / .reports / .organizations / .project_memories), (c) extend _LegacyMetadataFacade to delegate that aggregate's methods to the new repo, (d) move per-aggregate helpers with their owning aggregate (`_encode_session_cursor` / `_decode_session_cursor` → SessionRepository; `_build_transform_record` + validate_condition_sql import → TransformRepository per DWD-5 in DESIGN), (e) swap each backend/tests/repositories/test_<aggregate>_repository.py's repo fixture to construct the new per-aggregate class. Defines 8 narrow per-aggregate Protocols total (1 per aggregate; ProjectRepositoryProtocol from Phase 00 + 7 new). MetadataRepositoryProtocol stays as a deprecation alias per DWD-4. Repository.py is gutted in lockstep — once an aggregate's methods migrate to the new module, they are deleted from MetadataRepository (the facade owns delegation now). At end of phase, MetadataRepository is empty and removed; only _LegacyMetadataFacade remains. Steps glue: replace pytest.fail bodies in milestone-1 step bindings (given_seeded_for_aggregate, when_invoke_via_new_property, when_invoke_via_facade, then_invocations_match_fields, then_records_retrievable, when_first_access_facade, then_deprecation_warning_emitted, given_seeded_three_sessions, when_page_sessions_*, then_pagings_*, given_fk_violation_setup, when_create_transform_violating_fk, then_metadata_error_raised, given_project_dataset_transform, when_delete_project_new, then_dataset_gone, then_transform_gone) with real implementations.

phases.title: Phase 02 — Migrate use-case call sites onto new container properties (8 batches per ADR-020)
phases.scope: Per ADR-020 §Decision outcome — Phase B, in 8 sub-batches. For each aggregate, replace `repositories.metadata.<method>` with `repositories.<aggregate>.<method>` across consuming use cases. Sub-batch ordering matches the table in ADR-020 §Decision outcome (B1 Organization → B8 Transform), but the crafter may reorder by call-site count — the only constraint is one batch per atomic commit so each is independently revertable. Add the pytest-archon rule per DWD-7 (DESIGN's wave-decisions.md): `app.use_cases.*` MUST NOT import MetadataRepository or _LegacyMetadataFacade directly. Pre-Phase-03 the rule is `warn` (records hits without failing CI); Phase 03 promotes it to `error`. The grep audit at distill time identifies 40 use-case files importing from `app.repositories` (a superset of the actual `repositories.metadata` consumers; the actual count is ~25 per DESIGN §0). The crafter walks each batch's files, runs the existing per-use-case test suite (`cd backend && uv run pytest tests/use_cases/<aggregate>/`), and commits per batch.
phases.files_changed_estimate: B1 Organization (~2 files): backend/app/use_cases/auth/*. B2 Project Memory (~1 file): backend/app/use_cases/memory/get_project_memory.py. B3 Session (~3 files): backend/app/use_cases/session/{create_session,list_sessions,update_session,list_session_events}.py. B4 View (~5 files): backend/app/use_cases/view/*.py. B5 Report (~5 files): backend/app/use_cases/report/*.py (when present). B6 Project (~6 files): backend/app/use_cases/project/{create_project,delete_project,update_project,get_project,list_projects,export_dbt_project,project_service,provision_project_memory}.py. B7 Dataset (~5 files): backend/app/use_cases/dataset/{create_dataset_from_upload,list_datasets,update_dataset,get_dataset,search_datasets,dataset_service}.py + sql_access/{get_sql_access,_datasets,enable_sql_access,sync_sql_access,disable_sql_access,_engine,_context,regenerate_sql_credentials}.py. B8 Transform (~4 files): backend/app/use_cases/dataset/{update_transforms,create_transforms,preview_cleaning,_pipeline/ingestion}.py. backend/tests/architecture/test_use_cases_no_legacy_metadata_repo_import.py — NEW pytest-archon rule per DWD-7 (warn mode in this phase). ~25 use-case files edited; ~3-line edit per file (import + property name).
exit_criteria: Zero call sites in app/use_cases/* invoke `repositories.metadata` or import `MetadataRepository` / `_LegacyMetadataFacade` (verified by grep + by the new pytest-archon rule)
pytest-archon rule lands in warn mode (records zero hits)
All 11 milestone-1 scenarios still green

Each batch lands as a single atomic commit per CLAUDE.md commit conventions; the crafter can stop after any batch and the codebase is consistent
ac_references: DWD-6 (3-phase migration; Phase B = Phase 02 here)

phases.title: Phase 03 — Remove _LegacyMetadataFacade and promote architectural rule to error
phases.scope: Terminal phase per ADR-020 §Decision outcome — Phase C. (a) Delete backend/app/repositories/metadata/_legacy_facade.py. (b) Remove the `metadata_repository` key from RepositoryContainer._registry and the `.metadata` @property. (c) Delete `MetadataRepositoryProtocol` from backend/app/repositories/metadata/__init__.py (the deprecation alias from Phase 01). (d) Promote the pytest-archon rule from warn to error so any future use-case import of MetadataRepository / _LegacyMetadataFacade fails CI. (e) Migrate or delete backend/tests/repositories/test_metadata_exception_handling.py — its assertions about `handle_repository_exceptions` are reconstituted as a focused test on the lifted decorator at backend/tests/repositories/test_handle_repository_exceptions.py (or kept inside the per-aggregate suites, crafter judgement). (f) Amend ADR-020 status from Proposed → Accepted; record bead id; back-fill the open question in §Open questions about whether _LegacyMetadataFacade itself merited an archon rule (answer: it did, that rule was Phase 02's warn-mode rule). Replace pytest.fail bodies for milestone-2 steps (given_production_source_tree, when_scan_for_legacy_imports, then_no_legacy_imports, given_container_facade_removed, when_access_legacy_metadata_property, then_attribute_error, when_request_legacy_key, then_key_error, given_candidate_violator, when_run_archon_rule, then_archon_rule_fails, when_request_each_property, then_each_property_yields_instance) with real implementations.

deliver_dispatch_notes.paradigm: Python OOP (per CLAUDE.md backend conventions). Each new repository is a class with __init__(RestrictedSession). The decorator handle_repository_exceptions stays a module-level wrapper. _LegacyMetadataFacade is a class that composes 8 per-aggregate repos and forwards calls; per ADR-020 §Decision outcome step 5 it emits a DeprecationWarning on construction.
deliver_dispatch_notes.iron_rule: NEVER modify a failing acceptance test to make it pass. Each phase enables ONE scenario at a time per the Outside-In TDD outer loop. After 3 failed attempts, revert and escalate. CRITICAL for this refactor: NEVER edit a backend/tests/repositories/test_*_repository.py body — those are characterization tests. Only the conftest fixture is allowed to change. If a per-aggregate test fails after a fixture swap, the new repository's behaviour has diverged from the legacy class — fix the refactor, not the test.
deliver_dispatch_notes.tdd_cycle: RED_ACCEPTANCE → unskip one scenario (remove @pending or unpin via Examples row) → RED_UNIT (write failing unit test against new repo class) → GREEN (minimum implementation) → REFACTOR per RPP L1-L6 → next scenario.
context_handoff: ADR-020 (Proposed; promoted to Accepted in Phase 03)
